text:
Once upon a time, in the quaint city of Brookshire, there lived a small, bespectacled owl named Olive. She was renowned far and wide for her love of reading the daily newspaper — a source of endless amusement since she couldn't fly.

One autumn morning, as the golden leaves fluttered down from the rustling oaks, Olive noticed something peculiar. Her favorite newspaper had an extra page she hadn’t seen before. It was called “Fantastic Forests.” Curious, she opened it, discovering stories about mythical creatures and wonders of the untamed woodlands.

Every evening, Olive would listen to an audio book about adventures deep within trees and over brooks. She could picture herself soaring like a bat, wandering through ancient lamasery halls filled with glowing fungi, or navigating a maze of thorny vines.

One particular story captivated her: a tale of a wise old owl who had a map to a treasure hidden deep within a enchanted forest. Every night, the old
